title: JIT IR translation — arithmetic, comparison, and control flow
purpose: Translate the core BEAM opcodes (move, call, return, arithmetic, comparison, branching) into Cranelift IR. These are the most frequently executed instructions and the highest-value JIT targets.
task: Implement IR translation for ~20 core opcodes covering data movement, function calls, arithmetic, and control flow.

requirements.title: Data movement IR
requirements.spec: Translate move, swap, and register access to Cranelift IR.
acceptance: move generates load/store IR for x/y registers
Register file modeled as a memory region in IR
Test: compiled move produces correct register values

requirements.title: Arithmetic and comparison IR
requirements.spec: Translate add, subtract, multiply, div, rem, and comparison opcodes.
acceptance: SmallInt arithmetic compiles to native integer ops with overflow checks
Comparison opcodes compile to conditional branches

requirements.title: Control flow IR
requirements.spec: Translate call, return, label, and branching opcodes.
acceptance: Intra-module calls translate to direct jumps
Conditional branches (is_eq, is_ne, etc.) translate to branch instructions
Test: compiled function with branches executes correctly

boundaries: SHALL NOT translate heap-allocating opcodes (put_tuple, put_list) — those need GC integration (B-134)
SHALL fall back to interpreter for unsupported opcodes (mixed mode)
SHALL NOT implement tail call optimization in IR — basic calls first
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
